mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings contraction. Equity investors reprice downward in anticipation of lower corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 recovery phase with elevated unemployment at 5.2%
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fragile labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 2.28% represents accommodative monetary policy. A sustained level below 3% signals continued monetary support that reduces equity risk premiums and sustains positive equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5% annualized) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 3%, or Sharpe ratio of the signal < 0.4 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive due to improved earnings visibility. The interaction captures demand momentum that single-series analysis misses.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2004k units, RSAFS at $347.7B as of June 2010
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 117.8 reflects manufacturing capacity utilization. A 3-month declining trend in INDPRO signals weakening supply-side momentum and future profit margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors front-run earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following 3-month INDPRO declines are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 5.2% combined with stagnant payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals a labor market plateau. When UNRATE rises above 5.0% while PAYEMS growth slows below 0.1% monthly, equity investors reduce growth expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2%, PAYEMS at 132.573M as of July 2010
regime_hint: jobless recovery with stalled labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 191.1 combined with low federal funds rate (2.28%) creates negative real rates. This regime compresses bond yields and increases equity relative attractiveness, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as capital rotates from fixed income.
evidence_refs: fact:Negative real rates environment in mid-2010
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in negative real rate regimes are not significantly higher than positive real rate regimes (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 10975.7B reflects aggregate demand strength. A quarter-over-quarter growth rate below 2% annualized signals economic deceleration and triggers equity repricing downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following sub-2% GDP growth quarters are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly capture supply-side capacity and labor input. When both series show simultaneous acceleration (month-over-month growth > 0.3%), the interaction signals robust productive capacity expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both grow > 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Post-crisis recovery phase with improving supply metrics
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in rolling regressions.
